Site-specific hazard assessments (also called field-level hazard assessments) are in fact an 
administrative control used to address hazards that show up as a result of changing circumstances 
at a work site.
• Site-specific hazard assessments check for hazards before work starts at a site and at a

Are there different types of  
hazard assessments?
When Alberta’s legislation references hazard assessments, it refers to something called a formal 
hazard assessment. 
A formal hazard assessment takes a close look at the overall operations of an organization

answer: If a site-specific hazard assessment spots a hazard that was overlooked during the formal hazard assessment, the formal assessment should be updated to include it. Who should be involved? Those leading a site-specific hazard assessment must be competent in the task. They must understand the goal of the assessment, the work site environment, and the experience and skill levels of those who are working on site.

_meta.query_text: What are the site-specific hazards at Site A?
